findings.title: Test module loader leaves permanent stubs in sys.modules
findings.impact: _load_validate_module() installs fake yr/yr.datasystem CLI modules into the process-wide import cache and never restores the previous state. Future tests added to this file or run in the same Python process can silently import these stubs instead of the real CLI package, making failures hard to reason about and coupling otherwise unrelated tests to this loader.
findings.fix: Isolate the stubs with a scoped patch, for example unittest.mock.patch.dict(sys.modules, stubs) around spec.loader.exec_module(), or snapshot and restore the affected sys.modules entries in a finally block after loading the target module.

findings.title: Validator duplicates cluster config field names instead of reusing shared constants
findings.impact: The new validator hard-codes worker_nodes, worker_port, worker_config_path, and ssh_auth credential paths even though cli/common/constant.py already defines these keys as ClusterConfig values used by the deployment commands. If the cluster config schema is renamed or extended later, maintainers must update two independent vocabularies and the validator can drift from the rest of dscli.
findings.fix: Reuse ClusterConfig for the existing keys, or add a small shared schema/field helper in cli/common so generate/load/deploy/validate paths reference one source for the cluster config key names.
